items.title: Hadi: New opposition leader to be named before parliament convenes in June
items.summary: KUALA NERUS: Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang today said a new opposition leader will be appointed before parliament convenes in June. The Marang member of parliament said the decision would be finalised following thorough discussions involving all opposition parties. Larut member of parliament Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin is the current opposition leader.

items.title: CMCF urges media, public to put dignity before clicks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Communications and Multimedia Content Forum of Malaysia (CMCF) has urged media practitioners, content creators, and the public to exercise greater responsibility when reporting and sharing content involving road accidents. CMCF chief executive officer Mediha Mahmood said videos of the accident have been widely circulated on social media. "In situations involving loss of life, human dignity and compassion must come first.

items.title: French far-left MEP Rima Hassan to be tried in July over post on X
items.summary: PARIS: Rima Hassan, a French far-left member of the European Parliament, will face trial in July over a comment she posted on X last month about a 1970s attack on an Israeli airport, the Paris prosecutor's office said late on Thursday. "At the end of her custody, Rima Hassan was given a summons to appear before the criminal court on July 7, 2026, to be tried on charges of advocating terrorism committed online," the office said in an emailed statement. Jean-Luc Melenchon, the party's founder, said the proceedings against Hassan were politically motivated.

items.title: Socso to provide RM2,888 monthly lifeline to Klang rider’s widow and three kids
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 3 — The welfare of the widow and three children of a motorcyclist, killed in a road accident at Jalan Raya Barat, Klang, last Sunday, will continue to be safeguarded, as they qualify for a monthly Survivors’ Pension of RM2,888.17 from the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso). Perkeso Group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed said checks found that the late Amirul Hafiz Omar, 33, who had worked at TVH Malaysia Sdn Bhd for 15 years, was an active contributor under the Employees’ Social Security Act 1969 (Act 4). “This new Act officially provides social security protection under Perkeso to more than 1.64 million gig workers in Malaysia, ensuring compensation and welfare if they are involved in accidents while working,” he said.

items.title: After demanding a ‘life for a life’, Klang rider’s widow seeks tougher laws to end drugged driving
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 3 — The widow of a man who died after being hit by a driver under the influence of drugs in Klang recently hopes the government will consider amending existing penalties and laws to prevent similar incidents from recurring. Nor Nadia Abdul Majid, 32, the wife of the late Amirul Hafiz Omar, 33, said she hopes her husband’s death would serve as a lesson to society and lead to changes in current legislation. “There may be wisdom behind my husband’s passing.

items.title: High-speed chase through Ipoh neighbourhood ends after suspected cable thieves in BMW hit roadside pole
items.summary: IPOH, April 3 — A high-speed pursuit through the streets of Bercham ended in a crash and the arrest of two men early this morning, with police discovering suspected drugs and cable theft equipment in their vehicle. The incident unfolded at approximately 2:30 AM when a mobile patrol vehicle unit on routine patrol spotted a vehicle behaving suspiciously. Ipoh district police chief ACP Najib Hamzah said the suspects drove dangerously before losing control and colliding with a roadside pole.

items.title: Myanmar junta chief elected as president
items.summary: NAYPYIDAW: Myanmar's parliament elected junta chief Min Aung Hlaing as president on Friday, parliament said, with the ex-military commander set to maintain his rule in a civilian guise after snatching power by force five years ago. The vote on Friday across the upper and lower houses of parliament in the capital Naypyidaw saw Min Aung Hlaing secure a huge margin over the second-place candidate in a three-person race. "We hereby announce Senior General Min Aung Hlaing as president," parliament speaker Aung Lin Dwe announced from a stage in the parliament meeting hall.

items.title: Hadi: New Opposition leader to be named before June Parliament meeting
items.summary: KUALA NERUS, 3 April — A new Opposition Leader will be finalised before the Dewan Rakyat reconvenes in June, PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said today. “This matter requires consensus because it involves all opposition MPs, not just a single party,” Hadi told reporters at an event here today. Under parliamentary convention, the Opposition leader is typically the head of the largest opposition bloc.

items.title: Could a Mediation Centre cut case backlogs and legal costs? Azalina says possible if more disputes stay outside court
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — The government is mulling the possibility of establishing a Malaysia Mediation Centre (MMC) under the Legal Aid Department to strengthen out-of-court dispute resolution, reduce case backlogs and lower legal costs for the public. Law and Institutional Reform Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department, Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said, said the initiative aims to streamline mediation systems currently administered separately by various ministries and agencies through their respective tribunals. Azalina noted that not all individuals can afford litigation or expensive legal advice, citing the high cost of legal letters and services.

items.title: Shamsul Azri: Many civil servants yet to fully grasp Malaysia Madani concept
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar today said many civil servants have yet to fully grasp the Malaysia Madani concept, which he described as more than a slogan, but a guiding ethos to be practised comprehensively. While a large number of public officers have been exposed to Malaysia Madani initiatives, he said efforts must continue to ensure no one is left behind in understanding the concept introduced by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. "This exposure must continue.

items.title: PM opens first Madani Mart, aiming to ease cost of living
items.summary: KUANTAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today opened the very first Madani Mart at Indera Mahkota 8, marking a milestone in the country's effort to make daily necessities more affordable. Spearheaded by Yayasan Madani, Madani Mart is a community store concept offering daily necessities at affordable prices and easily accessible to the public, under the slogan 'Kedai Runcit Harga Rahmah' (Rahmah grocery store). Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail was also present.

items.title: Malaysian pharmacy group Big Caring files IPO draft prospectus
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The operator of Malaysia's largest pharmacy chain, Big Caring Group, is planning an initial public offering, a preliminary prospectus filed with the securities regulator showed. Maybank Investment Bank and RHB Investment Bank have been appointed as joint principal advisers, joint global coordinators, joint bookrunners, joint managing underwriters and joint underwriters for the IPO, the draft showed. AmInvestment Bank will serve as joint principal advisers, joint global coordinators, joint bookrunners and joint underwriters, while UBS will serve as joint global coordinators and joint bookrunners.

items.title: Australian drivers urged to refuel in cities as Mideast war deepens fuel crunch
items.summary: SYDNEY: Australia's government urged motorists on Friday to fill their cars at city petrol stations if they're planning long road trips over the Easter holiday, as rural towns face fuel shortages. Energy Minister Chris Bowen said hundreds of service stations had run out of diesel nationally and over a hundred were without unleaded petrol. On Friday, the first day of the Easter holiday, 182 service stations in the most populated state New South Wales had run out of diesel and 48 had no petrol of any kind, Bowen said.

items.title: Media Council, industry players asked to discuss WFH police for media practitioners
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Communications Ministry has called on the Malaysian Media Council (MMM) to hold discussions with local media companies and organisations to gather feedback on the implementation of a work-from-home (WFH) policy for the sector. Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the ministry's top priority in any decision on the policy is to safeguard the welfare of media personnel nationwide. Also present were Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong, MCMC chairman Tan Sri Mohamad Salim Fateh Din and SC chairman Datuk Mohammad Faiz Azmi.

items.title: Malaysia’s education initiatives bridge urban-rural gap in SPM 2025
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — The 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) results show the gap in National Average Grades (GPK) between urban and rural candidates narrowing, demonstrating the progress of Malaysia’s education democratisation initiatives. Director-general of Education Datuk Dr Mohd Azam Ahmad said the GPK for urban candidates was 4.40 in 2025, down from 4.47 in 2024, while the GPK for rural candidates fell to 4.81 last year from 4.95 the previous year. Meanwhile, Mohd Azam said the performance of candidates from Integrity Schools and Henry Gurney Schools recorded a 100 per cent pass rate in the 2025 SPM.

items.title: Thai PM blames stockpilers for fuel shortage
items.summary: BANGKOK: Thailand's prime minister slammed on Friday oil traders for "excessive profiteering", blaming those stockpiling fuel or smuggling it abroad for shortages that have driven prices steadily higher. "Authorities have found cases of fuel stockpiling and smuggling for sale in neighbouring countries," Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul said, adding the problem had cost the government 50 billion baht (US$1.4 billion). Fuel prices in Thailand have reached record highs, with unleaded gasoline at 57.51 baht (US$1.76) per litre and diesel at 47.74 baht (US$1.46).

items.title: Hassan Marican appointed crisis management task force chairman
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tan Sri Mohd Hassan Marican has been appointed chairman of the Crisis Management Task Force (PPPK) under the National Economic Action Council (MTEN) to coordinate government efforts to address the global energy crisis following the conflict in the Middle East. Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil said Hassan, who is also head of the special advisory body to the finance minister, will be responsible for reviewing daily actions that need to be taken and gathering feedback on West Asian issues. Fahmi also said the tasks of the team led by Hassan do not overlap with the special committee chaired by Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan, which is responsible for examining current economic developments following the conflict.

items.title: Italy extends fuel excise tax cuts for month
items.summary: ROME: Italy's government has extended by a month a temporary cut in fuel excise taxes that were due to expire on April 6, the economy minister said Friday. "We are extending the reduction of excise duties already in force, which would have expired on Easter Monday, until the first of May," Economy Minister Giancarlo Giorgetti said following a meeting of the council of ministers. He said the new decree-law -- to which some 500 million euros would be allotted -- also included a measure for agriculture businesses that would extend a 20 per cent tax credit.

items.title: Penang to study federal WFH guidelines before deciding on adoption
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Penang government will review and deliberate on the work-from-home (WFH) guidelines currently being developed by the federal government before deciding on their applicability at the state level. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the matter was raised during the recent menteris besar and chief ministers' meeting, which he attended together with the state secretary. "The state government will refine and discuss the work-from-home guidelines to be implemented by the federal government.

items.title: Kerjaya Prospek wins RM98.78mil hospital contract
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kerjaya Prospek Group Bhd's unit, Kerjaya Prospek (M) Sdn Bhd, has secured a RM98.78 million contract from Sena Letrik (M) Sdn Bhd for building, mechanical and electrical, as well as external works for a proposed private hospital project in Negri Sembilan. The contract covers the construction of a private hospital complex in Bandar Seremban Utama, Seremban 2, comprising an eight-storey hospital block with one basement level and supporting facilities. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Penang fixes school land tax at RM50 per lot
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A total of 522 schools in Penang will now pay a fixed land tax of RM50 per lot, replacing previously inconsistent charges. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the flat rate replaces earlier fees that ranged from RM1 to RM10, which often confused school administrators. "Schools can now review their tax bills via the PgLand portal and complete payments online or at land office counters across the state," he said.

items.title: Over 150 global investors explore opportunities in JS-SEZ
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor has positioned itself as a leading regional investment destination after engaging more than 150 high-potential global investors in Shanghai, China, recently. Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, who led the state delegation to Shanghai between April 1 and 5, said the investors' interest signalled growing confidence in the state's high-value opportunities through the Johor–Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ). "Johor's appeal as a gateway to Asean continues to strengthen," he said, adding that the state is ready to support investors seeking long-term, high-impact projects.

items.title: BOJ keeps rate‑hike door open even as Iran war squeezes firms
items.summary: TOKYO: The Bank of Japan will keep raising interest rates if its economic forecasts hold, a senior central bank official said, reinforcing a tightening bias even as fresh surveys show firms feeling the pinch of rising fuel costs linked to the Iran war. While higher oil prices pose risks to economic growth, they could also push up underlying inflation by raising long-term inflation expectations, said Koji Nakamura, the BOJ's executive director overseeing monetary policy, in parliament on Friday. "If our economic and price projections were to materialise, we will likely continue to raise interest rates," Nakamura said, adding that the degree and timing of future increases will depend on economic, price and financial conditions.

items.title: Hassan Marican leads Malaysia's new crisis task force to monitor fuel, food supply amid West Asia conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — Tan Sri Mohd Hassan Marican has been appointed as the chairman of the Crisis Management Task Force (PPPK) under the National Economic Action Council (MTEN) to coordinate the government’s efforts to address the global energy crisis following the conflict in West Asia. Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil said Hassan, who is also the head of the Special Advisory Body to the Minister of Finance, will be responsible for reviewing on a daily basis the actions that need to be taken, in addition to gathering feedback on West Asian issues. Fahmi also explained that the tasks of the team led by Hassan do not overlap with the special committee chaired by Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan, which is responsible for examining current economic developments following the conflict.

items.title: RTS Link on track for Jan 2027 opening; costs stable despite global energy crisis
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 3 — The global energy crisis triggered by the conflict in the Middle East has not impacted the progress of the Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System Link (RTS Link), Transport Minister Anthony Loke confirmed today. The RTS Link is entering a critical phase this year ahead of its launch in January 2027,” Loke told reporters following a briefing at the Bukit Chagar Customs, Immigration and Quarantine (CIQ) complex. Loke detailed the upcoming technical stages, which include system installation, acceptance testing, and dynamic testing.

items.title: RTS Link fare proposal to be presented by Q3, says Loke
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 3 — The proposed fare structure for the Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link is expected to be submitted for the Malaysian and Singaporean governments’ approval by the third quarter (Q3) of this year, said Transport Minister Anthony Loke. He said that the project’s joint operator, RTS Operations Pte Ltd, will present the proposed rates to both governments. “The Land Public Transport Agency in Malaysia and the Land Transport Authority in Singapore will need to approve the proposed fare rates before it is officially announced,” Loke told reporters after a project briefing at the CIQ complex here today.

items.title: Johor Baru–Singapore RTS Link hits 90pct completion, final phase underway
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Johor Baru–Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link has surged past 90 per cent completion, transforming from bare concrete into a near-ready transit hub. Transport Minister Anthony Loke led a rare media walkthrough of the Bukit Chagar Immigration, Customs and Quarantine (ICQ) complex, showing the project's shift from construction to operational readiness. At the immigration hall, e-gates are installed, and body scanners and baggage screening systems are being fitted, two months ahead of schedule.

items.title: RTS Link ticketing still being finalised, said Loke
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Ticketing details for the Johor Baru–Singapore RTS Link are still being finalised as the project enters its critical testing and commissioning phase, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said. The setback disrupted system integration and delayed key testing phases, including fault-free trial runs required before operations can begin. However, Loke stressed that while both the LRT3 project and the RTS Link involve the same contractor, no issues related to Siemens have arisen in the RTS project.

items.title: Gig Workers Act 2025: Now in effect, HR Ministry rolls out tribunal and council to enforce protection
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — A total of 76 individuals were appointed today to the Gig Workers Tribunal (TPG) and the Gig Advisory Council (MPGig), in a move to strengthen the enforcement of the Gig Workers Act 2025, which came into effect on March 31. “The tribunal has jurisdiction to hear, mediate and adjudicate disputes between gig workers and contracting entities,” he told reporters after presenting appointment letters and certificates to TPG and MPGig members here today. Ramanan said key issues discussed at the council meeting today included a proposal to introduce a minimum earnings rate, or floor price, for gig workers, to be determined through a consensus between the government, workers and contracting entities.

items.title: Best offer or best only for Sunway?
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: As the deadline for the voluntary conditional takeover offer by Sunway Bhd for IJM Corp Bhd approaches on April 6, the tone fromthe former has grown increasingly firm - defending its RM3.15 per share offer as the "best offer" while clearly signalling that it will walk away if the bid is rejected. Sunway maintained that RM3.15 is its best and final offer, with no intention of increasing it. In any corporate transaction, one fundamental truth remains: Sunway is the suitor, not IJM, the observer said.

items.title: Sunview secures contract worth RM1.96bil from TNB Genco for renewable energy project
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sunview Group Bhd's consortium, comprising its wholly-owned subsidiary, Fabulous Sunview Sdn Bhd, and Cypark Renewable Energy Sdn Bhd, has secured a contract from TNB Power Generation Sdn Bhd (TNB Genco) for a large-scale renewable energy project worth RM1.96 billion. Consortium Cre-Sunview is an unincorporated joint venture, in which Cypark Renewable Energy and Fabulous Sunview have 60 per cent and 40 per cent interests, respectively. Meanwhile, TNB Genco is a wholly owned subsidiary of Tenaga Nasional Bhd.

items.title: Ramanan says gig workers now have 'channel for justice' to protect income
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The National Gig Economy Consultation Council held its inaugural meeting today, prioritising a minimum "floor price" for earnings to protect 1.64 million gig workers. Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the floor price sets a baseline income, ensuring workers earn a fair minimum while providing a safety net under the Gig Workers Act 2025. "Datuk Dr. Mohd Ghazali will begin deliberations today.

items.title: 'I cannot grieve for long,' said widow of delivery rider killed in crash
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Refusing to remain in prolonged grief over the loss of her husband, Amirul Hafiz Omar, 33, who was killed after being hit by a speeding driver last Sunday, Nor Nadia Abdul Majid is determined to rebuild her life for the sake of their young children. My children will grow up; they need my attention and education. Earlier, Social Security Organisation group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed visited the family and handed over the Funeral Management Benefit (FPM) payment to Nor Nadia.

items.title: Myanmar's Min Aung Hlaing: General who made himself president
items.summary: But, one man loomed large over the general election held in Myanmar in December and January: junta chief Min Aung Hlaing. Min Aung Hlaing's shift from junta chief to the head of an administration with a civilian veneer comes after an election that analysts said was designed by the military to maintain the ruling generals' hold on power. Since the coup, Min Aung Hlaing has only had limited diplomatic contact with many of Myanmar's regional neighbours and has rarely spoken to non-state-controlled media.

items.title: DOE deputy director-general claims trial to taking RM21,000 bribes from waste company
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — Department of Environment (DOE) deputy director-general Dr Norhazni Mat Sari pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today, to six charges of receiving RM21,000 from a scheduled waste management company, four years ago. Deputy Public Prosecutor Datuk Ahmad Akram Gharib informed the court that the prosecution and defence had agreed to a bail of RM25,000 with one surety for all charges. “The prosecution is also requesting that the accused’s passport be surrendered to the court until the case is resolved,” said Ahmad Akram, who was also assisted by deputy public prosecutor Mohamad Fadhly Mohd Zamry.

items.title: IGP: Media-police collaboration vital for public trust
items.summary: Earlier, Khalid, accompanied by Special Branch director Datuk Ibrahim Darus, toured the operations of Media Prima Audio (MPA) and the News and Current Affairs Department (CNA). Also present were MPB Group chairman Datuk Seri Dr Syed Hussian Aljunid; MPB Group managing director Datuk Mohd Rafiq Razali; MPB Group chief financial officer Rosli Sabarudin; Media Prima Audio chief executive officer Nazri Noran; Media Prima Omnia chief operating officer Datuk Mohd Efendi Omar; Media Prima news and current affairs division group managing editor Jasbant Singh and deputy group managing editor of news and current affairs and New Straits Times group editor Farrah Naz Abd Karim The police's top brass included Integrity and Standard Compliance Department director Datuk Aida Abdul Hamid; Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M Kumar; and Crime Prevention and Community Safety Department director Datuk Seri Mohd Yusri Hassan Basri. Also present were Management director Datuk Seri Abdul Aziz Abdul Majid and Traffic Enforcement Investigation Department director Datuk Seri Muhammed Hasbullah Ali.

items.title: Tougher penalties needed for fatal drink-driving cases, said Azalina
items.summary: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the current penalties for such offences remain disproportionately low compared to the gravity of the crimes. She said relevant ministries should re-examine the legal provisions concerning fatal accidents caused by driving under the influence. "Otherwise, they might feel offenders can simply pay the fine.

items.title: An eight-year-old girl dies in Taiping, now her parents and stepparents will stand trial for murder
items.summary: TAIPING, April 3 — Four family members, including the biological mother, were charged in the Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of an eight-year-old girl last week. M. Savathri, 28, her husband M. Satis Kumar, 27, who is also the victim’s stepfather, the victim’s stepmother S.C. Veeraletchumy, 53, and stepfather M. Murali, 49, nodded after the charges were read out separately to them before Magistrate R. Prabakaran. However, no plea was recorded from any of the accused, as murder cases fall under the jurisdiction of the High Court.

items.title: Anwar begins one-day working visit in Kuantan
items.summary: KUANTAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today began his one-day working visit in Pahang by performing Friday prayers with the public at Saidina Umar Al-Khattab Mosque, Cenderawasih here. His arrival at 1.15pm with Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail was welcomed by Youth and Sports Ministry Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari, Deputy Economy Ministry Datuk Mohd Shahar Abdullah, and political secretary to the Prime Minister Datuk Ahmad Farhan Fauzi. The prime minister will conclude his visit in the evening by officiating the 2026 PMX Pendekar Madani Cup Silat Championship at Pahang Sports Complex (Sukpa).

items.title: T7 Global bags Berantai contract from Vestigo Petroleum
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: T7 Global Bhd has secured a contract from Vestigo Petroleum Sdn Bhd for offshore transportation and installation (T&I) works involving the FPSO Berantai. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, the group said the contract was awarded to its wholly owned subsidiary, Tanjung Offshore Services Sdn Bhd, via a letter of award dated March 18, 2026. "The contract covers the provision of offshore transportation and installation campaign for FPSO Berantai," it said.

items.title: Loke: RTS Link project on track despite Middle East tensions
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Despite the global geopolitical crisis stemming from conflicts in the Middle East, the Johor Baru–Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link remains on schedule, with contractors raising no concerns over cost escalations. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the RM5.24 billion project continues to progress as planned, with no requests submitted to revise contract values despite ongoing global economic and energy uncertainties. He said physical works for the cross-border rail link are nearing completion, with the project now entering a critical phase involving system installation, integration, and testing ahead of its targeted completion by the end of this year.

items.title: Cypark unit wins RM1.96bil Kenyir solar project
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Cypark Resources Bhd has secured a RM1.96 billion engineering, procurement, construction and commissioning contract (EPCC) for a large-scale floating solar photovoltaic (PV) project with battery energy storage system at Kenyir Lake, Terengganu. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the renewable energy firm said the contract was awarded by TNB Power Generation Sdn Bhd, a unit of Tenaga Nasional Bhd, to a consortium comprising its wholly-owned unit Cypark Renewable Energy Sdn Bhd (CRE) and Fabulous Sunview Sdn Bhd. "The EPCC contract covers the engineering, procurement, construction, testing and commissioning of a 595 megawatt alternating current floating solar PV plant, together with a battery energy storage system," it said.

items.title: Should the press shield Malaysians from overly graphic tragedy footage? Communications Ministry urges discussion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — The Malaysian Media Council (MMC) and the Malaysian Press Institute (MPI) need to hold discussions to identify appropriate ethics in reporting and broadcasting videos related to accidents or tragedies. Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil said the move was necessary following complaints received by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) regarding several video clips related to accidents that were posted in full without censorship by social media accounts owned by media companies. He said the act of uploading footage related to tragedies or accidents, such as vehicle dashboard camera footage without censorship, especially those with tragic and emotional elements, was feared to cause trauma to viewers.

items.title: Malaysia’s shrinking rice fields: Over 20,000 hectares lost to development, abandonment, and youths quitting farming
items.summary: “In addition, there are also abandoned rice fields because many younger families have not continued their rice farming and have migrated to the city,” he told Bernama while inspecting the project to reopen abandoned rice fields in Pahang Tua here today. For example, he said that PLS Group had entered into an agreement to rent abandoned rice fields in Pahang Tua here to be redeveloped. He said that indirectly, elderly rice farmers who are unable to cultivate their land will benefit, apart from reducing the number of abandoned rice fields.

items.title: May 7 for High Court to hear Najib’s stay bid on SRC International payment, decide costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — The High Court here today fixed May 7 to decide on costs and hear Datuk Seri Najib Razak’s application for a stay of the US$1.3 billion (RM5.24 billion) payment to SRC International Sdn Bhd. Judge Datuk Ahmad Fairuz Zainol Abidin, in allowing the company’s US$1.18 billion (RM4.75 billion) lawsuit against Najib, ordered him to pay the amount, in addition to US$120 million (RM483.97 billion) that he had received from the company into his personal bank account, hence bringing the total to US$1.3 billion. The court, however, did not fix the costs and granted Najib an interim stay on repaying the US$1.3 billion.

items.title: RM1.65 diesel for fishermen: Why LKIM is standing firm on the 50L daily limit
items.summary: IPOH, April 3 — The maximum limit of 50 litres of subsidised diesel per day at RM1.65 is currently sufficient for the daily operational needs of most fishermen in the country. He said the subsidy is not intended to cover the full operational costs of fishermen each time they go out to sea to catch fish. “In terms of catch, fish supply is sufficient.

items.title: AG: Dashcam video, witness testimony justify murder charge in Klang DUI crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — Citing clear evidence that a driver’s actions were “so imminently dangerous” that death was a near certainty, Attorney-General Tan Sri Mohd Dusuki Mokhtar today defended the decision to level a murder charge in the fatal Klang drink-driving crash. Speaking on the charge against R. Saktygaanapathy, Dusuki explained that the case meets the threshold for murder under Section 300(d) of the Penal Code, the New Straits Times reported. He noted the accused’s deliberate act of driving into the opposite lane at high speed, which created a highly perilous situation for the victim, delivery rider Amirul Hafiz Omar.

items.title: Private GPs welcome revised RM10–RM80 consultation fee after 30-year freeze
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — The Federation of Private Medical Practitioners’ Associations Malaysia (FPMPAM) has taken note of the gazettement of the revised general practitioner (GP) consultation fee schedule, which came into effect yesterday. Its president, Dr Shanmuganathan T.V. Ganeson, said the revision marks the first in over 30 years and should be viewed as a long-overdue correction to an outdated fee structure, rather than a conventional fee increase.

items.title: Selangor to launch special task force Monday to combat global energy crisis
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 3 — The Selangor government will announce the establishment of a special task force by Monday at the latest, to address current challenges and their impact on the people’s lives following the global energy crisis. Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said a meeting to finalise the establishment of the task force would be held today with members drawn from experts in various fields including economics, finance and industries such as oil and gas. He said programme and activity proposals would be submitted by departments and government-linked companies (GLCs), before being scrutinised for implementation by the task force.

items.title: MyDigital, Oracle team up to train 300,000 Malaysians in AI by 2029
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — MyDigital Corporation will collaborate with Oracle to train 300,000 Malaysians in artificial intelligence (AI) to enhance employability and support career growth by 2029. “I have launched the Oracle Skills Development Initiative (SDI), a collaboration between Oracle and MyDigital Corporation, at Oracle AI Experience Live 2026. He added that the training will encompass technologies such as Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI), OCI Generative AI, OCI Data Science, Oracle APEX, OCI DevOps and OCI Security.

items.title: Transit house caretaker and boat skipper among eight Indonesians under arrest for migrant smuggling in Pontian
items.summary: Pontian Police chief Supt Hadzrat Hussien Mion Hussain said the suspects, aged between 21 and 36, were picked up during the joint operation between the district police’s Criminal Investigation Division and the Kukup Region Two Marine Police. He said that during a Wednesday operation at 8 pm, five individuals were arrested, comprising a suspected caretaker of a transit house, a boat skipper, and three migrants. Hadzrat Hussien said the police also seized two boats and three mobile phones of various brands, with the total estimated at RM50,000.

items.title: JPJ eyes stricter DUI penalties, victim compensation after fatal crashes
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 3 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) has now begun a review and revision of several sections under the Road Transport Act (APJ) 1987 [Act 333] to allow drivers under the influence of alcohol or drugs to be subject to heavier penalties. JPJ director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli said JPJ, through its legal division, had begun a review and further study of two sections, namely Section 44 and Section 45 of APJ 1987. “The Minister of Transport has ordered the MOT and also the JPJ to review Section 44 and Section 45 under APJ.

items.title: Wang Kelian meth seizure: Three to face High Court over 510kg haul, penalty includes death
items.summary: KANGAR, April 3 — Three individuals, including a married couple, were charged at the Magistrate’s Court here today with trafficking 510 kilogrammes (kg) of methamphetamine in Wang Kelian last month. The accused, Mohd Rizuan Ramli, 37, and the married couple Muhammad Amir Aswad Ismail, 27, and Nur Atiqa Anis Ramadha Abdullah, 24, nodded in understanding after the charge was read before Magistrate Nurul Natasha Rizal. According to the charge, the three individuals were jointly accused of trafficking 510,000 grammes of methamphetamine at the main road near the mini tar junction at a TNB substation in Wang Kelian, Jalan Wang Kelian, Kaki Bukit near Padang Besar at about 9.30 am on March 24.

items.title: Police hunt man filmed in viral sexual harassment incident at Kajang MRT
items.summary: KAJANG, April 3 — Police are actively searching for a man captured in a viral video allegedly committing an act of sexual harassment at the Kajang MRT station. Kajang district police chief ACP Naazron Abdul Yusof confirmed in a statement late Thursday that a 23-second video of the incident began circulating on the social media platform Threads at approximately 9.00am. Efforts to track down and arrest the suspect are actively ongoing,” Naazron stated.

items.title: Four-day remand for meth-positive trailer driver in JB-Segamat crash that killed van driver and married couple
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 3 — The trailer driver involved in a fatal accident involving three vehicles at Kilometre 212, Jalan Johor Bahru-Seremban near Taman Golden Hill intersection, Gemas Baharu, yesterday evening was remanded for four days starting today until next Monday. Segamat district police chief, Supt. Mohd Jumazanzahir said in the incident yesterday evening, the victims who died were a married couple and the van driver.

items.title: Penang to decide on civil service WFH arrangements next week
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, April 3 — The Penang state government will discuss the implementation of work-from-home (WFH) arrangements for its civil servants next week, following a new federal directive aimed at reducing national fuel consumption. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow noted that Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had met with all chief ministers and menteri besar yesterday to discuss the matter. The state executive council and the state secretary will meet next Wednesday to determine the scope and any additional guidelines needed, Chow said.

items.title: ‘When Malaysians create, Malaysia will grow’: Economy minister says local TikTok content creators driving jobs, digital economy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — Malaysian youths are redefining what work and enterprise can look like in the digital age through content creation, livestreaming, affiliate marketing and digital entrepreneurship on TikTok and TikTok Shop. Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said young Malaysians are not just consuming digital content, but they are creating it, innovating through it, and building businesses around it. “This also includes opportunities linked to the digital economy, green economy, blue economy, circular economy and silver economy to diversify youth income sources,” he said.

items.title: Penang kicks off RM103m solar farm project that could power thousands of homes by 2027
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, April 3 — Penang has officially broken ground on a new RM103 million large-scale solar (LSS5) farm in Byram, Nibong Tebal, a project slated to generate 58,296 megawatt-hours (MWh) of clean energy annually starting from 2027. Chow said that the Byram solar farm complements other green initiatives in the state, including plans for floating solar installations at the Mengkuang and Teluk Bahang dams. Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow is briefed on the LSS5 project in Byram, Nibong Tebal on April 3, 2026.

items.title: Swiss national faces court after Customs find 3.3kg of heroin hidden in luggage at KLIA2
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 3 — An attempt to smuggle 3.3 kilogrammes (kg) of heroin worth nearly RM266,000 was foiled at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal 2 last February. KLIA Customs director Zulkifli Muhammad said the suspect, who was attempting to enter Malaysia from a Southeast Asian country, was stopped after a scan of a water hose reel box he was carrying revealed suspicious images. “A thorough inspection by the Customs enforcement team uncovered black packages concealed inside the water hose reel, believed to contain heroin,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: BDR doesn’t mean ‘Baring, duduk, rehat’: Chief Secretary warns civil servants as remote work policy starts April 15
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — The upcoming work-from-home (WFH) policy is not a licence for civil servants to neglect their duties, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar warned today. Set to begin on April 15, he said the WFH initiative demands the same discipline and productivity as working from the office and must be carried out with strict monitoring by department heads, Sinar Harian reported. “Output and productivity must remain the same, as officers implementing WFH need to have clear KPIs (key performance indicators) that are set in advance.” Shamsul Azri explained that the policy is part of a civil service reform that prioritises work results over mere physical attendance.
